action.hypothesis: JumpReLU at K=128 appears limited more by encoder capacity than by auxk tuning, so increasing expansion_factor from 8 to 16 while holding the improved LR=1.6e-3 and AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125 fixed may reduce proxy FVU below the current JumpReLU proxy anchor.
action.summary: Keep the best K=128 JumpReLU quality anchor fixed and begin the expansion-factor sweep with EXPANSION_FACTOR=16, since the recent auxk sweep underperformed and capacity is the next clean one-parameter probe.
action.self_review: This follows the runtime exploitation guidance and changes exactly one primary variable after two auxk reductions both degraded proxy quality. The main risk is extra capacity may not help at fixed optimization settings, but EF=16 is the most justified next test before broader architecture changes.
notes_to_memory: Rounds 14-15 showed AUXK_ALPHA=0 and 0.01 both worsening proxy FVU versus the K=128 JumpReLU anchor, so auxk reduction is not the near-term path to lower FVU.
This round shifts the single-variable sweep from regularization to capacity by testing EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 at the established K=128 JumpReLU baseline.
If EF=16 improves proxy quality without a major throughput drop, promote to full before considering smaller K or cost-focused follow-ups.
next_hypotheses: If EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then decide whether to stabilize EF further or tune LR on the higher-capacity model.
If EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 is worse or neutral, test EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 to map whether the current EF=8 is already near-optimal.
If both EF=16 and EF=4 fail, conclude local JumpReLU capacity sweeps are exhausted and return to architecture exploration or a fresh optimizer-level change.
